behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: this is about handoff between request construction and the API client, not just a pure function. REAL-INFRA/System triad is not explicitly required because payload inclusion can be verified with an in-process stub transport. LLM-DEP is disqualified by behavior text: it checks request payload completeness, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stub API client, invoke classify() with a taxonomy, and assert the outbound request payload contains every allowed label exactly as expected before send.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction boundary (API response parsing + taxonomy validation), so Integration is the lowest added reality check beyond unit-only. Higher levels rejected: behavior text disqualifies Agentic/System because correctness is local guardrail logic for an arbitrary invalid label and does not require real model quality or real network contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API response containing an out-of-taxonomy label (for example "biomedical") and verify classify() returns "unknown" to the caller.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly a deterministic normalization property on identical inputs, confined to one local transformation boundary with no required I/O or cross-module reality signal.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert the resulting label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live model calls and consistency of model-produced labels. MOCK-HIDE and LLM-DEP both apply: a stub cannot reveal real model variability. Workflow is disqualified by text because this is a single model-call behavior repeated, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated live classifications for the same document/taxonomy against the real provider, record raw responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff between token-budget policy, request builder, and dispatch gating. Integration catches whether over-budget input is truncated/rejected before send. System rejected: no explicit external contract or required round-trip side-effect readback. Agentic rejected: behavior text targets pre-dispatch enforcement, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, pass an oversized document, and assert the module applies policy (truncate/reject) and only dispatches compliant requests via a stub client.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real AI provider behavior (real tokenization/usage accounting on real model calls). A mock can mask budget overruns and provider-specific accounting differences. System triad cues exist (real external response contract), but LLM-DEP remains material because usage is tied to real model execution path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live calls with representative documents and assert provider-reported usage fields (input/total tokens) are <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is retry orchestration across modules (error mapping + backoff + retry loop), which can be validated with controlled stubbed rate-limit responses and timing hooks. System rejected by rejection-gate evidence: behavior text does not require validating real provider-specific error contract shape or external round-trip side-effect.
behaviors.test_description: Stub the API to return rate-limit errors first, then success; verify the module waits per policy, retries, and returns the eventual successful classification instead of surfacing an immediate error.
